summary: Created the mail-sync wrapper (flock-serialized, group-scoped, no mbsync -a), wired it into modules/home/default.nix, repointed notmuch preNew and aerc's $ keybind to it, and verified via nix flake check, home-manager build, and a two-run credential-free concurrency test.

artifacts.summary: New mail-sync wrapper: flock-serialized, gmail|logos|both allowlist, no -a, duplicate-UID detection
preNew repointed to mail-sync both || true
Credential-free stub-mbsync concurrency test proving serialization

completion_data.completion_summary: Created a single canonical, flock-serialized, group-scoped mail-sync wrapper structurally incapable of mbsync -a, wired it into the Home Manager import list, repointed both in-repo triggers (notmuch preNew, aerc's $ keybind) to it, and verified nix flake check, home-manager build --flake .#benjamin, and a credential-free concurrency test (run twice) all pass.

next_steps: Run 'home-manager switch --flake .#benjamin' to activate, then exercise mail-sync gmail/logos/both manually against the real account.
